A gray, medium- to coarse-grained rock composed of quartz, feldspar, and a mafic mineral. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.felsic.solid.granit1.spectrum.txt

A gray, medium-grained rock consisting of white feldspar grains, some of which contain red or pinkish cores, quartz, and minor biotite. Some of the feldspar appears to be perthitic. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.felsic.solid.granit2.spectrum.txt

A light gray, medium- to coarse-grained rock consisting of gray plagioclase, white feldspar, quartz, and chloritized biotite. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.felsic.solid.granit3.spectrum.txt

A pinkish medium-grained sample consisting of feldspar and quartz with minor biotite. Some areas of the sample show granophyric texture and a few vugs are present. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.felsic.solid.granit5.spectrum.txt
A reddish, banded, porphyritic rock with the phenocrysts small (< 1 mm) and variable in size. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.felsic.solid.rhyoli1.spectrum.txt

The sample consists of a dark gray, fine-grained groundmass, with yellow (hypersthene?), dark green (augite?) and white (plagioclase) phenocrysts. The sample is also slightly vuggy. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.intermediate.solid.andesi2.spectrum.txt

A dark gray, porphyritic and vuggy rock. The phenocrysts are plagioclase laths, orangish-yellow olivine and opaques. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.intermediate.solid.andesi4.spectrum.txt

A dark gray, medium-grained rock composed of plagioclase, a dark mafic phase and minor biotite. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.intermediate.solid.diorite1.spectrum.txt

A dark gray medium-grained rock consisting of gray plagioclase, pinkish potash feldspar, quartz, biotite, scattered sulfides, and a black mafic phase. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.intermediate.solid.granod1.spectrum.txt

A dark gray medium-grained rock consisting of lightgray plagioclase grains, white to pinkish feldspar (plagioclase >> alkalifeldspar), green epidote or apatite, a black mafic phase and quartz. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.intermediate.solid.granod2.spectrum.txt

A purplish-gray, medium-grained granular rock composed of feldspar, a greenish to black mafic mineral and opaques. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.intermediate.solid.monzon1.spectrum.txt
This sample appears to be a black homogenous glass with good conchoidal fracture. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.intermediate.solid.obsidi1.spectrum.txt

A light gray medium-grained rock composed of pink feldspar, grayish plagioclase, quartz and biotite. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.intermediate.solid.qmonzo1.spectrum.txt

A dark gray fine-grained rock spotted with whitefeldspar grains in a dark gray groundmass with scattered mafics, opaques, and sulfides. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.intermediate.solid.syenit1.spectrum.txt

A medium-grained gray rock composed of whitish plagioclase, quartz, biotite, and a black mafic mineral. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.intermediate.solid.tonali1.spectrum.txt

The hand sample consists of very dark gray large tabular plagioclase with interstitial bluish-green granulated feldspar groundmass, with some opaque areas approx. 1-3 mm in size scattered through thesample. The Marcy anorthosite from which this sample was taken is described in: Buddington, A. F., 1938, Adirondack igneous rocks and theirmetamorphism: Geological Society of America Memoir No. 7, p. 19-33 and 258-259. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.mafic.solid.anorth1.spectrum.txt

A gray, porphyritic, vuggy or vesicular rock with green phenocrysts ranging in size from < 1 mm to 2 mm and making up < 5% ofthe whole rock. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.mafic.solid.basal7.spectrum.txt
A greenish-black aphanitic rock. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.mafic.solid.basal1.spectrum.txt

A light gray porphyritic rock with green olivine phenocrysts 1 to 2 mm in size making up about 3% of the rock, set in a gray, very fine-grained groundmass. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.mafic.solid.basal10.spectrum.txt

The sample has a glassey rind over a more crystalline interior. It is approximately 4 x 2 cm, light gray on "weathered" surfaces and dark gray on fresh surfaces. This vesicular sample possesses small phenocrysts < 1 mm which make up about 10% of the rock. The phenocrysts consist of plagioclase laths and olivine, with plagioclase much more abundant than olivine. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.mafic.solid.basal2.spectrum.txt
Meerdink et al. 2019, Baldridge et al. 2009
